channel.title: NYT > Business
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: All Those A.I. Note Takers? They’re Making Lawyers Very Nervous.
item.description: A trendy productivity hack, A.I. note takers are capturing every joke and offhand comment in many meetings. They could also potentially waive attorney-client privilege.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kessler

item.title: Frontier Jet Hits Person on Runway During Takeoff at Denver Airport
item.description: The plane reported an engine fire and aborted its takeoff. The people on board were safely evacuated.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Chris Hippensteel
item.media:description: A Frontier Airlines plane at Denver International Airport this month.

item.title: Which Trump Tariffs Are in Place, in the Works or Ruled Illegal
item.description: The president has reworked his tariffs repeatedly — sometimes because they have been declared illegal — with more updates still to come.
item.dc:creator: Lazaro Gamio and Tony Romm

item.title: How ‘The View’ Landed at the Center of a Free Speech Battle
item.description: The Trump administration’s focus on the show is testament to the enduring influence of an old-fashioned broadcast TV program started 29 years ago.
item.dc:creator: John Koblin and Jim Rutenberg
item.media:description: The hosts of ABC’s “The View,” from left, Whoopi Goldberg, Sara Haines, Joy Behar, Ana Navarro, Sunny Hostin and Alyssa Farah Griffin.

item.title: For Struggling JetBlue, Spirit’s Demise May Offer an Opportunity
item.description: JetBlue Airways is adding flights at Spirit Airlines’ former home airport as it tries to turn a profit again.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: With the closure of Spirit Airlines, JetBlue Airways is now the largest airline at Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood International Airport, near Miami.

item.title: Taiwan’s Plastic Habit Collides With Shortages Caused by a Faraway War
item.description: Taiwan, one of the world’s biggest plastic users, is facing supply disruptions caused by the war in Iran. The crunch is already trickling down into everyday life.
item.dc:creator: Xinyun Wu, Meaghan Tobin and An Rong Xu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People use more plastic in Taiwan than almost anywhere else in the world. The average person uses at least one new plastic shopping bag a day, adding up to 10 billion plastic bags a year.

item.title: Retirees Expect Their Home to Be a Financial Safety Net. They Shouldn’t.
item.description: Older homeowners often don’t see the value of, or can’t afford to, maintain and renovate their homes of many years. And that can mean thousands lost when they sell.
item.dc:creator: Martha C. White

item.title: When Mark Consuelos Wants to Escape New York, He Goes Here
item.description: “The midcentury architecture is fascinating. It’s also a place where I’m outside almost every waking hour of the day.”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: Live With Kelly and Mark (TV Program)

item.title: China’s Exports and Imports Set Records in April Amid High Energy Costs
item.description: The country’s exports surged and its trade surplus with the United States widened ahead of President Trump’s scheduled visit next week to Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:credit: Gilles Sabrié for The New York Times
item.media:description: At the Beijing auto show in April, which featured hundreds of electric cars. Chinese electric vehicles are being exported in record numbers.

item.title: Norovirus Outbreak Sickens 115 on Cruise Ship
item.description: More than 100 passengers and 13 crew members on the Caribbean Princess reported being ill, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: The Caribbean Princess left Port Everglades in Fort Lauderdale, Fla., on April 28 and is expected to return to Florida on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Targets Iran’s Missile and Drone Program With Sanctions
item.description: The new measures are focused on Chinese companies that supply Iran’s military with materials to make drones.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Friday.

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses Lucky Strike of Building a Bowling Monopoly
item.description: The suit, filed by bowlers from several states, accuses Lucky Strike Entertainment of driving up prices and diminishing customers’ experiences across the country.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:credit: Evan Sung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lucky Strike’s alley in Times Square is one of the company’s more than 360 locations in North America.

item.title: Justice Dept. Settles Case Against Provider of Meat Industry Data
item.description: Under the agreement, Agri Stats would pay a fine and broaden whom it sold its pricing data to. The department said the move would help reduce food costs.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Draper
item.media:description: The Justice Department said meat processors had used information exchanges run by Agri Stats to limit supply and raise prices.

item.title: Trump Plans to Fire F.D.A. Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Dr. Makary has been a supporter of the Make American Healthy Again Movement but made some enemies in the administration over vaping, the abortion pill and rejections of new drugs.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Maggie Haberman
item.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary, commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration, at the White House last year.

item.title: What to know about the report.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Ember

item.title: How Prepared Are We for A.I. Layoffs?
item.description: Our chief economics correspondent, Ben Casselman, describes how A.I.-related layoffs are testing the resilience of the government’s safety net programs.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Trump’s Latest Tariff Setback Looms Over China Talks
item.description: For the president, a major legal defeat could undercut his leverage as he prepares to head to Beijing next week for high-stakes trade negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Ana Swanson
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader, in 2025. Tariffs are expected to be a major topic when they meet next week in Beijing.

item.title: Iran Says It Seized Oil Tanker Listed by U.S. as Sanctions Violator
item.description: A maritime tracking company said the move was likely performative, given the Chinese-owned ship has an “established history within the Iranian trade ecosystem.”
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Friday.

item.title: ABC Accuses Government of Violating First Amendment
item.description: The network’s argument, made to the F.C.C., is the most aggressive posture taken yet by a television network toward the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Jim Rutenberg and John Koblin
item.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The filing on Friday was registered on behalf of a single ABC station in Houston and involved a minor regulatory dispute over the talk show “The View,” which regularly hosts politicians and cultural figures.

item.title: Meta’s Embrace of A.I. Is Making Its Employees Miserable
item.description: As it adapts to the artificial intelligence era, the company is pushing many of its 78,000 workers to use the technology, and preparing to lay some of them off.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang, Eli Tan and Kate Conger

item.title: You May Be Owed a Tax Refund From the Covid Era
item.description: A court ruling found that the I.R.S. shouldn’t have charged penalties and interest during the pandemic. But the government may appeal the decision.
item.dc:creator: Ann Carrns

item.title: What’s Happened So Far at the Musk v. OpenAI Trial
item.description: Elon Musk’s court battle against OpenAI has laid bare tensions between the start-up's leaders and raised questions about the billionaire’s motivations.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Mike Isaac
item.media:description: Outside the federal courthouse in Oakland, Calif., last week during the Elon Musk v. OpenAI trial.

item.title: Federal Reserve Turns Focus to Inflation as Job Market Stabilizes
item.description: April’s report showed employers added more jobs than expected, supporting the central bank’s view that it can afford to hold interest rates steady.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Higher prices could eventually temper consumer spending, causing a slowdown that could result in job losses.

item.title: With fuel costs soaring, Trump insists the economy is strong.
item.description: White House aides have sounded bullish on the jobs report, even as warning signs mount amid the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kevin Hassett, the director of the White House National Economic Council, was optimistic about the jobs report due Friday.

item.title: What to know about the report.
item.description: Employers added 115,000 jobs and the unemployment rate remained at 4.3 percent despite higher energy prices and instability spurred by the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Ember
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: ‘Fired From Retirement’: Financially Insecure, Older Americans Return to the Grind
item.description: People return to work for many reasons, but the biggest is financial need. As costs remain high, more retirees could be looking to re-enter the job market.
item.dc:creator: Kurtis Lee
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pat Archer retired several years ago but found that her Social Security and small pension couldn’t keep up with her bills. She found part-time work — in a retirement home.

item.title: Stocks Rise For Sixth Consecutive Week, Buoyed by Strong Jobs Data and Earnings
item.description: Investors weighed data on the American jobs market and conflicting signals about status of the conflict in the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How Energy Prices Are Driving Demand for Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
item.description: Already having faced an energy crisis five years ago, more European consumers believe they are “one Trump-ignited war away” from crushing costs.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: David Payr for The New York Times
item.media:description: A worker in Vienna installing a heat pump for the company Heizma, which said it had record sales in March and April.

item.title: Trade Court Rules Trump’s 10% Global Tariff Is Illegal
item.description: A panel of federal judges found that President Trump could not legally impose the tariff on most imports.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Tom Brenner for The New York Times
item.media:description: A court found that President Trump had wrongly invoked a decades-old trade law when he applied the duties in February.

item.title: U.S. Debt Hits a Concerning Milestone, and Experts Say Trump’s Policies Could Worsen It
item.description: The debt is outgrowing the size of America’s economy. The president’s policies could accelerate the country’s fiscal headaches, experts say, unless policymakers intervene.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The reaction to the U.S. debt has appeared muted on Capitol Hill, despite years of hand-wringing among Republicans, in particular, about the need for fiscal restraint.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX Plans $55 Billion Investment to Make A.I. Chips
item.description: The rocket company’s new semiconductor factory, called Terafab, is part of the billionaire’s increasing efforts to dominate artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Steve Lohr
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A SpaceX Starship rocket near the Starbase in Boca Chica, Texas.

item.title: Why Stocks and Bonds Are Responding Differently to the Iran War
item.description: Stock investors are betting that companies will make enormous profits, despite the war. But investors in bonds, including U.S. Treasuries, have other concerns.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People attending a pro-government demonstration in Tehran last month. The S&P 500 has hit new highs since the start of the war in Iran.

item.title: Shell Reports Nearly $7 Billion Profit After Oil Prices Surged Amid U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The oil giant’s earnings in the first three months of the year were more than double the previous quarter’s and follow similarly strong results of European rivals.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Shell reported huge profits for the oil company in the first three months of the year.

item.title: Why Investors Are Still Betting Big on Kalshi
item.description: The popular prediction market platform has just raised a new funding round that values it at $22 billion, double what it was worth in December.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Christine Zhang
item.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kalshi, a prediction market platform, just raised a new funding round as it continues to grow rapidly.

item.title: Kalshi, the Prediction Market, Raises $1 Billion at a $22 Billion Valuation
item.description: The platform for betting on sports, politics, the weather and more has raised $1 billion and sees big financial firms as a key source of growth.
item.dc:creator: Michael J. de la Merced
item.media:description: Kalshi sees big financial firms as a major source of growth.

item.title: McDonald’s, Burger King and Taco Bell Report Strong Sales Amid Rising Consumer Costs
item.description: A 35 percent rise in gas prices didn’t dampen demand at restaurants like McDonald’s, Burger King and Taco Bell, which reported increased sales.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: John Taggart for The New York Times
item.media:description: McDonald’s has been leaning into its value meals to entice consumers.

item.title: Help! We Got to the Gate in the Nick of Time, but Missed Our Flight.
item.description: A California couple rushed through Heathrow to catch their connecting flight, arriving with minutes to spare. But British Airways had already given away their seats.
item.dc:creator: Seth Kugel

item.title: Is It an Art Gallery? A Museum? A Theater? A Dream?
item.description: The Ministry of Awe, a new immersive experience in a former bank in Philadelphia, aims to help locate the wondrous in the everyday.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski and Michelle Gustafson
item.media:credit: Michelle Gustafson for The New York Times
item.media:description: In the exhibit “Heavens,” Imani Lee Williams plays the role of High Priestess.

item.title: After Stumbles, Technology Meant for Self-Driving Cars Finds a Second Act
item.description: After a crush of promises in 2016 that autonomous cars would soon rule the road, companies whose tech underpinned them have shifted to other industries, like managing shipyards and city traffic.
item.dc:creator: John R. Quain
item.media:credit: Ilvy Njiokiktjien for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Port of Rotterdam in the Netherlands, where ground-penetrating radar is being tested for automation systems.

item.title: Oil Prices Waver Amid Mixed Signals on Renewed U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Investors waited for news on the situation in the Middle East, as the U.S. and Iran gave mixed signals on peace negotiations.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: War and Energy Shortages Boost China’s Influence in Asia
item.description: The war in Iran has left China’s neighbors appealing for help, handing Beijing the kind of sway it has long sought.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Oil storage tanks at a depot in Hong Kong. Shipments of Chinese oil products to select countries have offered relief during the war and underscored how Beijing engenders good will in times of crisis, experts say.

item.title: The ‘Perfect Storm’ Hanging Over Britain’s Public Debt
item.description: Bond investors are expected to scrutinize the results of critical local elections on Thursday as the war in the Middle East adds inflation pressure on the government.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:credit: Joseph Horton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Investors are betting the Bank of England will approve several interest rate increases this year, reversing previous expectations that rates would come down.

item.title: Anthropic’s C.E.O. Says It Could Grow by 80 Times This Year
item.description: The chief executive, Dario Amodei, said the rapid growth had exponentially increased the start-up’s need for more computing power.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha
item.media:description: Anthropic’s chief executive, Dario Amodei, in January. On Wednesday, he spoke at his company’s annual developer conference.

item.title: JPMorgan Tried to Settle Harassment Accusations Against Executive
item.description: The bank offered $1 million before a former employee filed a lawsuit against a woman who was an executive director on his team, two people briefed on the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland
item.media:description: A New York lawsuit accuses JPMorgan Chase of allowing one of its investment bankers to make sexual advances and racially motivated comments to a lower-ranking employee.

item.title: GameStop’s CEO Ryan Cohen Baffles Fans With eBay Bid
item.description: Investors questioned Ryan Cohen’s latest gambit after he gave an evasive CNBC interview and a once-loyal supporter sold his GameStop shares.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch

item.title: Attack on French Cargo Ship Highlights Continued Risks in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. Central Command and the ship’s owner disagreed on whether the vessel coordinated safe passage with the United States military.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Oman on Wednesday.

item.title: Rupert Murdoch, David Zaslav and More Media Moguls Remember Ted Turner, CNN’s Founder
item.description: The CNN founder’s death was met with encomiums from leaders in the media industry, which he helped reinvent. “He will be remembered for generations,” said one.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Brian F Alpert/ZUMA Press Wire, via Shutterstock
item.media:description: Ted Turner in the 1980s.

item.title: Higher Gas Prices Are Hitting Lower-Income Americans the Hardest
item.description: People with lower incomes are driving less and devoting more of their budgets to transport as gas prices soar.
item.dc:creator: Talmon Joseph Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gas prices have soared since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Disney Posts Strong Earnings Despite Slowdown in Park Visitors
item.description: Disney parks are closely watched as a bellwether for consumer confidence.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Disney’s former chief executive Bob Iger and current chief executive, Josh D’Amaro, at Disneyland’s 70th anniversary commemoration last year.

item.title: Ted Turner, Creator of CNN and the 24-Hour News Cycle, Dies at 87
item.description: As one of the most important figures in media history, he oversaw a vast cable empire of news, sports and entertainment channels.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Kandell
item.media:description: Ted Turner was a visionary media mogul who dominated the cable television industry, and extended his reach into movies, major league baseball and even yachting.
